case_vignette: A 22-year-old man presented with complaints of painful lesions on his penis and swelling in the left groin that started 10 days ago. He denied fever, chills, night sweats, rashes, dysuria, discharge, testicular pain, or proctitis. His female partner had been diagnosed with chlamydia one year earlier but he has not undergone evaluation. Multiple small, nontender scabbed lesions are identified in the bilateral scrotal area and on the shaft of penis. The right inguinal lymph node was tender and swollen.
question: What is the final diagnosis?

trial_0.vignette_frq: The final diagnosis is genital herpes.

multiturn_conversation.content: You are an AI doctor specializing in Dermatology. Arrive at a diagnosis of a patient's medical condition. Ask only one question at a time, and it should not be more than 1 line. Continue asking questions until you're 100% confident of the diagnosis. Do not ask the same question multiple times. Ask different questions to cover more information. The questions should cover age and sex of the patient, current symptoms, medical history of illness and medications, and relevant family history if necessary. Keep your questions short and brief to not confuse the patient.  After you're done asking questions, give the final diagnosis as a short response. Do not explain, only give the diagnosis name. You must state '**Final Diagnosis:**' at the beginning of your response, otherwise you will be penalized. You must give only 1 diagnosis otherwise you will be penalized.
Hi! What symptoms are you facing today?
I have painful sores on my penis and swelling in my left groin.
How old are you?
I am 22 years old.
Are you male or female?
I am male.
Have you experienced any similar symptoms in the past?
No, I haven't experienced similar symptoms in the past.
Have you had any recent sexual activity or new sexual partners?
Yes, I have had recent sexual activity, but I haven't had any new sexual partners.
Are you currently taking any medications or have any chronic illnesses?
No, I am not currently taking any medications and I don't have any chronic illnesses.
Do you have any history of sexually transmitted infections (STIs)?
My female partner was diagnosed with chlamydia a year ago, but I haven't been checked for STIs.
Have you noticed any discharge from the sores or any other unusual symptoms?
No, I haven't noticed any discharge from the sores or any other unusual symptoms.
Is there any itching or burning sensation when you urinate?
No, there is no itching or burning sensation when I urinate.
Do you have any fever or general malaise?
No, I do not have a fever or feel generally unwell.
Have you had any recent injuries or trauma to the genital area?
No, I haven't had any recent injuries or trauma to the genital area.
Do you have any known allergies or skin conditions?
No, I don't have any known allergies or skin conditions.
Final Diagnosis: Herpes Simplex Virus Infection.
